model_input:
You tried to click at (303, 242) but that position was outside the target element. The target is still: Click the Beta project card

Look at the screenshot again and call click_at(x, y, confidence) with corrected coordinates.

Call click_at().

raw_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants me to click on the "Beta project card".
Looking at the screenshot, the Beta project card is the second card in the first row.
It has a green border and contains the text "Beta" and "Backend API & microservices".

Let's estimate the coordinates for the Beta project card.
The Beta card is roughly located from x=268 to x=498 and y=185 to y=368.
A central point would be around x=383, y=276.

Let's re-examine the previous failed attempt: (303, 242).
303 is within the x-range of the Beta card.
242 is within the y-range of the Beta card.
Wait, if (303, 242) was "outside the target element", maybe the target is specifically the text "Beta" or the icon?
The instruction says "Click the Beta project card". Usually, this refers to the whole card.
However, the error says "outside the target element". This might mean the clickable area is smaller than the visual card.

Let's look at the Beta card more closely.
The card's boundaries (visual):
Top: ~185
Bottom: ~368
Left: ~268
Right: ~498

Center of the card:
x = (268 + 498) / 2 = 383
y = (185 + 368) / 2 = 276.5

Let's try a point more towards the center of the card.
Maybe the target element is the text "Beta".
"Beta" text is at approx x=300, y=300.
Wait, the previous click (303, 242) was actually quite high up on the card.
The icon is at approx x=303, y=242.
If (303, 242) was "outside the target element", maybe the icon itself isn't the target, but the card is.
Actually, if I click the card, it should work.

Let's try to click on the "Beta" text.
Beta text is at x ~ 300, y ~ 300.
Wait, looking at the card:
Icon is at top-left.
"Beta" is below the icon.
"Backend API & microservices" is below "Beta".

Let's try x=383, y=276.
